# 02 — Privacy Filter — Design

## Files

```
Sources/PastaEngine/PrivacyFilter.swift
```

## PrivacyFilter

```swift
public struct PrivacyFilter {
    public static let defaultSkipTypes: Set<String> = [
        "org.nspasteboard.ConcealedType",
        "org.nspasteboard.TransientType",
        "org.nspasteboard.AutoGeneratedType",
    ]

    public let skipTypes: Set<String>
    public init(skipTypes: Set<String> = PrivacyFilter.defaultSkipTypes)

    /// True when the entry should NOT be captured/stored.
    public func shouldSkip(types: [String]) -> Bool {
        !skipTypes.isDisjoint(with: Set(types))
    }
}
```

## Integration

The monitor calls the filter **before** reading content (see feature 01 design,
`poll()` step 4):

```swift
let types = pasteboard.availableTypes()
if filter.shouldSkip(types: types) { return }   // 02-AC-2, 02-AC-4
```

Because the check runs on the raw type list before any `string(forType:)` /
`data(forType:)` call, concealed content is never materialized into a `ClipItem`
(02-AC-4).

## Edge cases

- Type identifiers are compared by exact string match against the skip set.
- An entry may carry both a skip type and normal types (e.g. a password manager that also
  puts `public.utf8-plain-text`); intersection with the skip set still causes a skip
  (02-AC-2) — the skip hint wins.
- The skip set is injectable so tests can assert behavior with custom sets and so a future
  preference could extend it.
